task_completion.description: The agent opens the docker-compose.yml file located in /home/ga/projects/shadow-bug/.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., nano, vim, VS Code) or terminal command (e.g., cat) showing the contents of docker-compose.yml.

task_completion.description: The agent modifies docker-compose.yml to include an anonymous volume for /app/node_modules to prevent shadowing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The docker-compose.yml file is shown with a new volume entry like '- /app/node_modules' under the volumes section for the application service.

task_completion.description: The agent starts the Docker container using docker-compose.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal showing the execution of 'docker-compose up', 'docker compose up', or similar commands, and the container starting.

task_completion.description: The agent verifies the container is running and the application has not crashed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing 'docker ps' with the container 'shadow-app' running, or docker-compose logs showing the Node.js server successfully listening on port 3000.

task_completion.description: The agent verifies the application responds correctly at http://localhost:3000.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal showing a 'curl http://localhost:3000' command returning 'Hello from Docker!', or a web browser displaying 'Hello from Docker!' at localhost:3000.

task_completion.description: The agent ensures that dependencies are not installed on the host machine.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output of 'ls' or 'tree' in the project directory showing no 'node_modules' folder, or the absence of any 'npm install' command executed on the host.

integrity.description: The agent did not run 'npm install' on the host machine.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal screenshot shows 'npm install' being executed directly in the host's /home/ga/projects/shadow-bug/ directory.

integrity.description: The HTTP response is genuine and not faked.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Hello from Docker!' response is the output of a real curl command or browser navigation, not an 'echo' command or manually typed text.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely edited the docker-compose.yml file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file is edited using a real text editor or standard shell commands, rather than just claiming it was edited without showing the process.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is '/home/ga/projects/shadow-bug'. The container name is 'shadow-app'. The expected port is 3000. A successful GET request to http://localhost:3000 must return 'Hello from Docker!'.
